deja: Okay. So. Good news: no drones down here.
The canopy's too dense, the humidity screws with their sensors.
Bad news: the sensors aren't the only things in the water.
elias: The conversion program doesn't work on them.
maya: The path runs north of the deep channel.

elias: Sometimes you don't need to talk.
elias2: My knees are about forty years older than the rest of me.

pre: Elias saw it before any of us did.
He just pushed Deja toward the bank.
during: Elias was between us and it.
He'd made the calculation before he moved.
post: Deja didn't cry until we were clear of the trees.
Maya's hands didn't stop shaking for an hour.
It felt like something I could do.
loss_notice: The bayou keeps its own ledger.
The party feels lighter in a way that has nothing to do with weight.

deja: He knew the bayou better than any of us.
Southeast. Follow the old power lines.
